steps.title: Summarize each text
steps.content: Text 1: digital art "isn't really art" because it requires less skill than physical art — the technology does most of the work. Text 2: digital artists need art fundamentals plus mastery of sophisticated software, and often draw on paper first, so the digital tools are a craft of their own.

steps.title: Predict the response
steps.content: Author 2 would push back on Text 1's "less skill" claim by insisting that digital art still demands real artistic skill, just with different tools.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'digital ... can still be displayed traditionally': display format is not what Text 1 disputes; Text 1 disputes skill. Off topic. ✗
  • Choice B — 'much harder to use a tablet and stylus ... than ... paint and a brush': Text 2 never claims digital is harder, only that it requires real skill. Overreach. ✗
  • Choice C — 'digital art requires artistic abilities and skill even if it employs less traditional tools': directly answers Text 1's "not real art / less skill" charge using Text 2's points. ✓
  • Choice D — 'most digital artists don't think fundamental drawing skills are important': contradicts Text 2, which stresses art fundamentals and drawing on paper first. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
